title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken

seo.title: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken Recipe | Crispy & Authentic

description: Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a beloved dish featuring incredibly crispy fried chicken pieces coated in a luscious, sticky, sweet, and spicy sauce. This popular Korean street food and late-night snack offers an irresistible symphony of textures and flavors that will tantalize your taste buds. Enjoy restaurant-quality Yangnyeom chicken right in your own kitchen with this authentic recipe.

ingredients.notes: cut into pieces, patted very dry

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Pat chicken pieces very dry with paper towels. Season with salt, black pepper, ginger powder, and garlic powder. In a large bowl, whisk together all-purpose flour, cornstarch, and baking powder. Gradually add cold water to the dry mixture, stirring until a smooth, thin batter forms. Add the seasoned chicken to the batter, ensuring each piece is fully coated.

instructions.title: Make the Yangnyeom Sauce
instructions.text: In a small saucepan, combine gochujang, soy sauce, rice vinegar, honey or corn syrup, brown sugar, minced garlic, grated ginger, sesame oil, and water. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly until the sugar dissolves and the sauce thickens slightly, about 3-5 minutes. Remove from heat and set aside.

instructions.title: First Fry
instructions.text: Heat frying oil in a large pot or deep fryer to 170°C (340°F). Carefully add chicken pieces, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot. Fry for 6-8 minutes until light golden brown and cooked through. Remove chicken with a slotted spoon and place on a wire rack to drain excess oil.

instructions.title: Second Fry for Crispy Perfection
instructions.text: Increase oil temperature to 185°C (365°F). Return the partially cooked chicken to the hot oil and fry for another 3-5 minutes. This double-frying method creates an incredibly crispy crust. The chicken should be deep golden brown and extra crunchy. Remove and drain again on a wire rack.

instructions.title: Coat the Chicken in Sauce
instructions.text: While the chicken is still hot, transfer it to a large mixing bowl. Pour the prepared Yangnyeom sauce over the fried chicken. Toss gently but quickly to ensure every piece is evenly coated with the sticky, flavorful sauce.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Arrange the sauced chicken on a serving platter. Sprinkle generously with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions. Serve immediately while hot and crispy for the best experience.

instructions.title: Enjoy Your Delicious Meal
instructions.text: Gather your loved ones and enjoy this authentic taste of Korea. This dish pairs wonderfully with a side of pickled radish or a refreshing beer, making it perfect for any occasion.

tips: For ultimate crispiness, always double-fry your chicken. The first fry cooks it through, and the second fry at a higher temperature creates that signature crunchy crust.
Gochujang is crucial for authentic flavor. If you prefer less spice, you can reduce the amount of gochujang and add more honey or corn syrup to maintain sweetness.
Yangnyeom chicken is best enjoyed immediately. If you have leftovers, store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator and reheat gently in an oven or air fryer to regain some crispness.
article:
Korean fried chicken has taken the world by storm, captivating palates with its unparalleled crispiness and diverse range of delectable sauces. Among the many beloved variations, Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken stands out as a true icon, offering a perfect harmony of sweet, spicy, and savory flavors that are utterly addictive. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that authentic Korean fried chicken experience in your own kitchen, you've come to the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to master the art of making this quintessential Korean dish, ensuring every bite is a crispy, saucy delight.

## What is Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken?

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken translates literally to "seasoned sauce Korean fried chicken," and it's the epitome of what makes Korean fried chicken so special. Unlike its Western counterparts, Korean fried chicken is typically double-fried, a technique that renders the skin incredibly thin, light, and shatteringly crisp, even after being coated in sauce. The "yangnyeom" refers to the signature sticky, glossy, and intensely flavorful sauce that coats each piece of chicken. This sauce is a complex blend, usually featuring gochujang (Korean chili paste) for its distinctive heat and depth, along with sweeteners like honey or corn syrup, soy sauce for umami, garlic, ginger, and often a touch of vinegar for tang.

The result is a culinary masterpiece: chicken that boasts an almost glass-like crunch, enveloped in a vibrant, balanced sauce that hits all the right notes – sweet, spicy, tangy, and savory. Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken isn't just a meal; it's a cultural phenomenon, deeply embedded in Korean social life, famously paired with beer in a combination known as "chimaek." Its unique texture and unforgettable flavor profile have made it a global sensation, inspiring countless home cooks to try their hand at this irresistible dish.

## Ingredients You Need

To create your own batch of authentic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken, you'll need a combination of fresh chicken, pantry staples, and key Korean ingredients.

**For the Chicken:**
*   **Chicken:** 2-3 pounds (about 1-1.5 kg) chicken wings, drumsticks, or boneless thighs, cut into bite-sized pieces.
*   **Marinade:**
    *   1 tbsp soy sauce
    *   1 tsp minced ginger
    *   1 tsp minced garlic
    *   1/2 tsp salt
    *   1/4 tsp black pepper
    *   1 tbsp rice wine or mirin (optional)
*   **Batter/Coating:**
    *   1 cup all-purpose flour
    *   1/2 cup cornstarch or potato starch (crucial for crispiness)
    *   1 tsp baking powder
    *   1/2 tsp salt
    *   1 cup cold water or sparkling water (for extra crispness)
*   **Frying Oil:** 4-6 cups neutral oil (canola, vegetable, peanut)

**For the Yangnyeom Sauce:**
*   2 tbsp gochujang (Korean chili paste)
*   1 tbsp gochugaru (Korean chili flakes, optional, for extra heat)
*   2 tbsp soy sauce
*   2 tbsp rice vinegar
*   3 tbsp honey or corn syrup (adjust to desired sweetness)
*   1 tbsp brown sugar
*   1 tbsp minced garlic
*   1 tsp grated fresh ginger
*   1 tbsp ketchup (for tang and body)
*   1 tsp sesame oil
*   2-3 tbsp water or chicken stock (to thin sauce if needed)

**Garnish (Optional):**
*   Toasted sesame seeds
*   Chopped green onions

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps carefully to achieve perfectly crispy and saucy Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken.

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Pat the chicken pieces thoroughly dry with paper towels. In a large bowl, combine the chicken with the marinade ingredients (soy sauce, ginger, garlic, salt, pepper, rice wine). Mix well and let it marinate at room temperature for 30 minutes, or refrigerate for at least 1 hour (up to 4 hours).
2.  **Make the Batter:** In another large bowl, whisk together the flour, cornstarch, baking powder, and salt. Gradually add the cold water (or sparkling water), mixing until a smooth batter forms. The consistency should be like a thick pancake batter – not too runny, not too thick.
3.  **First Fry:** Pour the frying oil into a large, heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, filling it about 3-4 inches deep. Heat the oil to 325°F (160°C). Dip each marinated chicken piece into the batter, ensuring it's fully coated, then carefully lower it into the hot oil. Fry the chicken in batches, ensuring not to overcrowd the pot, for 6-8 minutes, or until lightly golden and cooked through. The internal temperature should reach 165°F (74°C). Remove the chicken with a spider or slotted spoon and place it on a wire rack set over a baking sheet to drain.
4.  **Prepare the Sauce:** While the chicken is frying, prepare the Yangnyeom sauce. In a small saucepan, combine all the sauce ingredients: gochujang, gochugaru (if using), soy sauce, rice vinegar, honey, brown sugar, minced garlic, grated ginger, ketchup, sesame oil, and water. Bring to a simmer over medium-low heat, stirring constantly until the sugar dissolves and the sauce thickens slightly, about 3-5 minutes. Keep warm.
5.  **Second Fry:** Increase the oil temperature to 375°F (190°C). Return the partially fried chicken to the hot oil for a second fry. Fry in batches again for another 3-5 minutes, or until the chicken is deeply golden brown and exceptionally crispy. This double-frying step is essential for that signature crunch.
6.  **Sauce and Serve:** Immediately transfer the hot, crispy chicken to a large bowl. Pour the warm Yangnyeom sauce over the chicken and toss vigorously until every piece is evenly coated. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and chopped green onions, if desired, and serve immediately.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving perfect Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken consistently requires attention to a few key details:

*   **Double Frying is Non-Negotiable:** This is the secret to the ultimate crispy texture that holds up to the sauce. The first fry cooks the chicken, and the second fry crisps it to perfection.
*   **Pat Chicken Dry:** Excess moisture on the chicken prevents the batter from adhering properly and results in less crispy chicken.
*   **Use a Thermometer:** Accurate oil temperature control is crucial. Too low, and the chicken will be greasy; too high, and it will burn before cooking through.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Pot:** Frying too much chicken at once will lower the oil temperature significantly, leading to soggy chicken. Fry in small batches.
*   **Cornstarch/Potato Starch is Key:** Incorporating these starches into your batter creates a lighter, crispier coating than flour alone.
*   **Adjust Sauce to Taste:** Feel free to modify the levels of gochujang, honey, or vinegar to suit your personal preference for spice and sweetness.
*   **Toss Immediately Before Serving:** To maintain maximum crispness, toss the hot, freshly fried chicken in the warm sauce right before serving.

## Serving Suggestions

Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a versatile dish that can be enjoyed in many ways. The traditional pairing, and arguably the best, is with *mu-samu*, thinly sliced pickled daikon radish, which offers a refreshing, tangy crunch that perfectly cuts through the richness of the fried chicken and sauce.

Other popular serving suggestions include:
*   **Steamed White Rice:** A simple accompaniment to soak up any extra sauce.
*   **Kimchi:** The fermented cabbage adds another layer of authentic Korean flavor.
*   **Coleslaw or Fresh Salad:** Provides a cool, crisp contrast.
*   **Cold Beer or Soda:** The ultimate beverage pairing for a classic "chimaek" experience.
*   **As a Main Meal:** Serve with sides for a complete dinner.
*   **As an Appetizer:** Perfect for sharing with friends and family.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken recipe is superb, there are many ways to customize it to your liking:

*   **Chicken Cuts:** While wings and drumsticks are traditional, boneless chicken thighs or even chicken breast cutlets can be used for easier eating. Just adjust frying times accordingly.
*   **Spice Level:** For a milder sauce, reduce the amount of gochujang and omit the gochugaru. For extra heat, increase both!
*   **Sweetness:** Adjust the amount of honey or corn syrup to achieve your desired level of sweetness. Some recipes also use brown sugar or even fruit purees for sweetness.
*   **Gluten-Free:** Substitute soy sauce with tamari and use a gluten-free all-purpose flour blend for the batter. Ensure all other ingredients are certified gluten-free.
*   **Vegetarian/Vegan:** While not traditional, you can adapt the sauce to coat fried tofu, tempeh, or even thick slices of king oyster mushrooms for a plant-based alternative. The texture won't be identical, but the flavor can be delicious.
*   **Garlic Lover's Version:** Double the amount of minced garlic in the sauce for an extra pungent kick.
*   **Honey Garlic:** For a less spicy, more sweet-savory profile, you can increase the honey and reduce the gochujang.

No matter how you choose to make it, mastering Yangnyeom Korean Fried Chicken is a rewarding culinary journey. The combination of crispy chicken and that irresistible sweet, spicy, and savory sauce is a testament to the magic of Korean cuisine. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to impress yourself and your loved ones with this unforgettable dish!
